integration_validation_v2.gap: Drift-guard deliverable (diagnostics_manual_drift_guard) is a Planned manual row only; the named DiagnosticsManualDriftGuard is a string literal, not code. No negative check is actually implemented or run; proof is in-memory struct membership.
integration_validation_v2.evidence: src/backend/handshake_core/src/model_manual/content.rs:2636-2655 (diagnostics_manual_drift_guard status Planned, 'runs as a test-time check' that does not exist); DiagnosticsManualDriftGuard appears only as the name field, no type anywhere (grep hit only content.rs); tests/model_manual_tests.rs:718-726,848-872 (asserts Planned status + None route, never executes a guard)

integration_validation_v3.evidence: Executable guard: src/atelier/model_manual_merge.rs:517-596 run_manual_drift_guard (wired-unresolved-surface, missing-manual-row, orphan-row, id-collision) + wired_surface_fingerprint + record_manual_drift_guard_run (stateful HBR-MAN-001 version-bump check across persisted runs, table per migrations/0122:39-58, EventLedger mirror). Negative tests detect INJECTED drift: tests/atelier_model_manual_merge_tests.rs:609-650 (exact drift kinds on synthetic drifty manual, shipped manual clean), :709-792 (3 guard runs persisted to managed PG, ManualVersionNotBumped fires on fingerprint diff, clears after bump, ledger event), :795-840 (clean real-manual run persisted).
integration_validation_v3.gap: Wired rows declaring no ipc_channel/tauri_command bypass surface-resolution branch by design; mitigated by impl-citation assertions in model_manual_tests.rs:872-902.
integration_validation_v3.basis: v3 adversarial per-MT review of remediated deliverable + runtime proof via full-crate managed-PG sweep and kernel-builder fresh-DB proof logs
